human:
POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180] would be great to a have a test verifying it works
probably `ShowCreateUtilTest`
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds an 8-arg overload of `buildShowCreateMaterializedTableRow` with `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` flags, allowing callers to suppress those clauses from SHOW CREATE output. The existing 6-arg overload delegates to the new one with `true, true`, preserving backward compatibility. One production caller (`ShowCreateMaterializedTableOperation`) uses the 6-arg version and is unaffected.

POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:146] The new 8-arg overload lacks Javadoc for the two new boolean parameters. Add `@param` tags explaining `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` and document the intended use case (platform-level deployments that fix these values at the platform level).
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:146] The 6-arg overload is `public static` but the new 8-arg overload is package-private. Consider making the 8-arg overload `public` as well so that external platform integrations (the stated use case) can invoke it directly without reflection.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180-185] When both flags are `false`, the output will contain an extra blank line between `START_MODE` and `AS` (since `extractStartMode` always appends `"\n"` and the conditionals contribute nothing). Consider whether a trailing blank line in the rendered DDL is desirable, or whether the newline after START_MODE should also be conditional on at least one of the flags being true.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:150] Good use of `@CsvSource` to cover all four combinations of the two boolean flags — clean and concise parameterization.
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180] The conditional emission pattern is clean and straightforward. No issues with the logic itself.
v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds an 8-arg overload of `buildShowCreateMaterializedTableRow` with `includeFreshness` and `includeRefreshMode` boolean flags, allowing callers to suppress FRESHNESS and REFRESH_MODE clauses from `SHOW CREATE MATERIALIZED TABLE` output. The existing 6-arg overload delegates to the new method with `true, true`, preserving backward compatibility.

POINTS:
- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:131-157] The delegation pattern (6-arg → 8-arg with default `true, true`) follows the existing convention in this class (`extractFormattedOptions` has a similar 2-arg → 3-arg delegation). Clean approach.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtil.java:180-187] The conditional emission logic is correct: each included clause appends its own `\n`, so there are no orphan trailing newlines or missing separators. Output with `(true, true)` is identical to the original.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:151-195] The new parameterized test using `@CsvSource` correctly exercises all 4 flag combinations. The test dynamically builds the expected string to match the flags — clean design.

- [flink-table/flink-table-api-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/table/api/internal/ShowCreateUtilTest.java:160-169] Minor style note: the `createResolvedMaterialized` helper creates a materialized table with no options, partition keys, or distribution (all null/empty). This is appropriate for a focused test, but note that it means the test does not validate that freshness/refresh mode flags work correctly alongside WITH clauses, partitioning, or distribution — those combinations are covered indirectly by existing tests through the 6-arg delegation path.

- No production callers currently exercise the new 8-arg overload (the only caller, `ShowCreateMaterializedTableOperation`, still uses the 6-arg version). This is fine for an additive internal API, but if the intent is for platform-level deployments to suppress these clauses, consider whether `ShowCreateMaterializedTableOperation` should eventually accept and forward these flags from a configuration option.
